meta.name: Sweden
meta.aboutStudying: Sweden has a long tradition of high-quality, publicly funded higher education. EU/EEA students study for free — no tuition fees at all. Courses are conducted at a high level of English and Swedish degrees are globally respected. The country consistently tops global quality-of-life rankings and its universities lead in sustainability, technology and life sciences.

tips.title: Free for EU Students
tips.text: EU/EEA students pay zero tuition fees at Swedish public universities. Non-EU students pay SEK 80,000–170,000/year (roughly €7,000–15,000). The Swedish government offers Institut Français-style scholarships and universities offer merit-based aid. Cost of living is moderate — Stockholm costs SEK 10,000–14,000/month for a student.

tips.title: Dark Winters, Bright Summers
tips.text: Winters in Sweden are cold and very dark — Stockholm gets barely 7 hours of sunlight in December. However, Sweden compensates with long, light summer evenings, fantastic skiing, and some of the world's most beautiful autumn landscapes. Most students adapt quickly and find the contrast invigorating.

tips.title: Sustainability First
tips.text: Sweden is the world leader in sustainability — it aims to be fossil-fuel free by 2045. Swedish universities have embedded environmental thinking into almost every curriculum. The culture of fika (coffee breaks), flat hierarchies and work-life balance makes Sweden one of the most pleasant countries in which to study.

universities.name: KTH Royal Institute of Technology
universities.desc: Scandinavia's leading technical university — ranked top 100 globally and the birthplace of innovations from Bluetooth to Skype.

universities.name: Stockholm University
universities.desc: Sweden's second-largest university and a world leader in natural sciences, law and social sciences.

universities.name: Lund University
universities.desc: Sweden's highest-ranked university — in the QS world top 100 across almost every subject, with one of Europe's most international campuses.

universities.name: Uppsala University
universities.desc: Scandinavia's oldest university and a global top-100 institution — Uppsala has produced 8 Nobel laureates and Carl Linnaeus studied here.

universities.name: University of Gothenburg
universities.desc: Sweden's second most comprehensive university, known for outstanding humanities and marine research.

universities.name: Chalmers University of Technology
universities.desc: One of Europe's leading technical universities — a QS world top-80 institution producing many of Sweden's top engineers.

universities.name: Linköping University
universities.desc: A modern problem-based learning university that consistently rises in global rankings, with a major presence in Swedish industry.

universities.name: Umeå University
universities.desc: One of Sweden's larger comprehensive universities — a young, dynamic campus in northern Sweden's cultural capital.

universities.name: Karolinska Institutet
universities.desc: One of the world's most respected medical universities — the institution responsible for awarding the Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine.

universities.name: Stockholm School of Economics
universities.desc: Scandinavia's leading business school — consistently ranked in the top 30 European MBAs and producing Sweden's top business leaders.

universities.name: Malmö University
universities.desc: A young, urban university with a strong focus on social sustainability, urban planning and digital media.

universities.name: Stockholm University – Law Faculty
universities.desc: Sweden's most prestigious law faculty, producing the majority of Sweden's judges, attorneys and senior officials.

universities.name: Swedish University of Agricultural Sciences
universities.desc: The world's leading university for sustainable agriculture, forestry and ecology — uniquely placed across four campuses.

universities.name: Karlstad University
universities.desc: A smaller comprehensive university in western Sweden offering many English-taught programmes in a safe, pleasant city.

universities.name: Örebro University
universities.desc: A dynamic modern university in the heart of Sweden, with growing international recognition in law and business.

universities.name: Mid Sweden University
universities.desc: A dual-campus university in central Sweden with specialist programmes in ecotourism, sports and digital media.

universities.name: University West
universities.desc: Sweden's university for work-integrated learning — a unique model where all programmes include real workplace experience.

universities.name: Halmstad University
universities.desc: A small coastal university focused on innovation and health research, with a pleasant student environment.

universities.name: Linnaeus University
universities.desc: A modern university formed from two merged institutions, with a strong international focus and large study-abroad programme.

universities.name: University of Borås
universities.desc: Specialist in textiles, library science and nursing — a compact, internationally recognised professional university.

universities.name: University of Gävle
universities.desc: A growing university in central Sweden with a strong emphasis on sustainable development and construction engineering.

universities.name: Dalarna University
universities.desc: A dual-campus university in the scenic Dalarna region, with many online and distance programmes.

universities.name: Södertörn University
universities.desc: A progressive social sciences university in southern Stockholm, focusing on Baltic and Eastern European studies.

universities.name: Red Cross University College
universities.desc: A specialist nursing and humanitarian studies college with a proud 150-year tradition connected to the Red Cross movement.

universities.name: Jönköping University
universities.desc: Sweden's leading private university with an outstanding reputation for entrepreneurship and the Jönköping International Business School.

universities.name: Stockholm School of Economics – Executive
universities.desc: Executive education arm of SSE — one of Scandinavia's most prestigious and expensive MBA and executive programmes.

universities.name: Swedish School of Sport and Health Sciences
universities.desc: One of the world's oldest sport and physical education universities — a unique specialist institution in central Stockholm.

universities.name: Stockholm Business School
universities.desc: Part of Stockholm University, SBS offers internationally focused business programmes in one of Europe's most connected capitals.

universities.name: Konstfack University of Arts
universities.desc: Sweden's leading arts and design university, with outstanding programmes in ceramics, jewellery, textiles and industrial design.

universities.name: KTH School of Architecture
universities.desc: Scandinavia's top architecture school, affiliated with KTH Royal Institute of Technology and consistently ranked globally.

universities.name: Stockholm College of Osteopathic Medicine
universities.desc: A specialist healthcare college offering degrees in osteopathy, physiotherapy and manual medicine.

universities.name: Ersta Sköndal Bräcke University College
universities.desc: A Christian-inspired healthcare and social work college with a long tradition of training nurses and social workers.

universities.name: Nackademin University of Applied Sciences
universities.desc: A vocational higher education institution specialising in IT, security and technical programmes in Stockholm.

universities.name: Blekinge Institute of Technology
universities.desc: A specialist technical university with a strong global reputation in software engineering and game development.

universities.name: University of Skövde
universities.desc: A compact university known across Europe for game development and bioinformatics, in a safe Swedish city.

universities.name: Lund University – Medical Faculty
universities.desc: Sweden's top medical faculty and one of Europe's leading academic medical centres — home to pioneering research.

universities.name: Karolinska Institutet – Biomedical Sciences
universities.desc: One of the world's top environments for biomedical research — KI's focus on translational science is globally renowned.

universities.name: Kristianstad University
universities.desc: A modern university in southern Sweden with a particularly strong food and meal science programme.

universities.name: Lund University – Faculty of Engineering
universities.desc: The Faculty of Engineering at Lund — LTH is Sweden's top engineering school and one of Europe's most respected.

universities.name: KTH – School of EECS
universities.desc: KTH's School of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science — ranked top 10 in Europe for CS and IT.

universities.name: Umeå University – Medical Faculty
universities.desc: Northern Sweden's only medical faculty, with strong research in cancer, Alzheimer's and infectious diseases.

universities.name: Stockholm University – Social Science
universities.desc: One of Sweden's top faculties for psychology, sociology and criminology — located on SU's leafy Frescati campus.

universities.name: Linköping University – Institute of Technology
universities.desc: Sweden's third-largest engineering school and a major centre for computer science, materials science and aerospace.

universities.name: Chalmers – Architecture and Civil Engineering
universities.desc: Chalmers' architecture and civil engineering school — ranked among the top 50 architecture schools in the world.

universities.name: University of Gothenburg – Academy of Music and Drama
universities.desc: One of Scandinavia's leading arts academies for music, drama, film and opera performance.

universities.name: Uppsala University – Faculty of Science and Technology
universities.desc: Uppsala's technical faculty — home to Sweden's best physics and chemistry research programmes.

universities.name: Sophiahemmet University College
universities.desc: A specialist nursing and radiotherapy university college with a 140-year tradition of compassionate healthcare education.

universities.name: Nordica University College of Art
universities.desc: A boutique private arts college in Gothenburg specialising in contemporary fine arts and digital media.

Stockholm.desc: Scandinavia's largest city and Europe's fastest-growing capital — spread across 14 islands, home to 5 world top-200 universities and an extraordinarily high quality of life.
Stockholm.studentLife: Outstanding — KTH and Stockholm University campuses are among Europe's most vibrant.
Stockholm.transport: Excellent metro (Tunnelbana), buses, trams and ferries; Arlanda airport 40 min by train.
Stockholm.nightlife: Expensive but world-class — legendary clubs, jazz bars, outdoor summer terraces.

Uppsala.desc: Scandinavia's oldest and most academic city — dominated by Uppsala University and SLU, with a medieval cathedral, countless student nations and a fiercely proud academic tradition.
Uppsala.studentLife: Legendary — the Student Nation system is unlike anywhere else in the world.
Uppsala.transport: Pendeltåg commuter trains to Stockholm (40 min) every 10 minutes.
Uppsala.nightlife: Driven entirely by student nations — incredibly lively and affordable.

Gothenburg.desc: Sweden's second city and the home of Volvo and Chalmers — a vibrant port city with excellent universities, a lively arts scene and Scandinavia's best food.
Gothenburg.studentLife: Excellent — two major universities, huge student population, very liveable city.
Gothenburg.transport: Extensive tram and bus network; ferry to Denmark (3 hrs); direct trains to Stockholm.
Gothenburg.nightlife: More intimate than Stockholm — cosy bars, live music and summer island hopping.

Lund.desc: A quintessential Swedish university town — compact, cycling-friendly and completely dominated by Lund University, which is Sweden's highest-ranked institution and one of Europe's finest.
Lund.studentLife: Exceptional — 43,000 students in a town of 91,000 creates a uniquely intense student culture.
Lund.transport: 15 min to Malmö; 40 min to Copenhagen by Øresund Bridge; excellent local buses.
Lund.nightlife: Student union-focused nightlife — AF-borgen is legendary; also easy to reach Malmö or Copenhagen.

Linköping.desc: A growing university city in southern Sweden — home to Linköping University and major aerospace companies including Saab, with an outstanding student quality of life.
Linköping.studentLife: Excellent — problem-based learning at LiU creates an unusually collaborative student atmosphere.
Linköping.transport: Hourly trains to Stockholm (2 hrs) and Gothenburg; excellent local buses.
Linköping.nightlife: Lively student scene around Corson; improving food and music venues.

cityCards.desc: Scandinavia's capital — 14 islands, 5 top universities, extraordinary quality of life and free tuition for EU students.
Scandinavia's oldest and most academic city — the unique student nation system and Uppsala University make this unmissable.
Sweden's second city — Chalmers, Volvo headquarters, Scandinavia's best food and an excellent student life.
Sweden's top university in a compact medieval town — 15 minutes from Copenhagen.
Rising aerospace and tech university city — Saab and Linköping University together make this a unique destination.
